author: @paulmaydaynight9925
text: it's booting... just install nomachine & use that from another  nomachine  device over the lan/wan router

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That sounds awesome, i guess it connects via remote from another device? I will take a look for sure,  thanks mate!

replies.author: @paulmaydaynight9925
replies.text:
​@MyNameIsLukey yeah, just install it on any device (comes in android, arm linux, windows versions) then it will find all the other nomachine *on the same lan/wan,* click & go, any machine to any other remote machine, simples.

so good you can (vlc) stream 1080p video from the broke screen device -assuming it can properly play video locally- directly over 100Mb/s + lan/wlan/wlan perfectly, unlike the usual messy vnc etc.

author: @myarvind
text: What if the Disk is encrypted? I had a new laptop where by default there is some encryption active. Removing the HDD & fixing it to another Laptop did not work.

replies.author: @TheGigashadow
replies.text: That is absolutely not correct, only the OS can have encryption not the drive itself (for most Windows based computer on the market for the general public), i guarantee that your bios was not configured properly or it was something else just as basic that was the problem, not "encryption"!

replies.author: @hootsmin
replies.text: @TheGigashadow You get hardware based encryption which is usually on the drive itself in the hardware or you get software based encryption which is done by the OS.  In short - your not correct either.

replies.author: @TheGigashadow
replies.text: @hootsmin This is true, there are programs used for this. However they are generally not standard in your average commercial Window based computer on the market that most people will purchase. This is why i made my point of "for most Windows based computer on the market for the general public". Software such as this is for the most part an aftermarket products except in some rare instances.

replies.author: @paulmaydaynight9925
replies.text: if it's new then it's under warrantee, if it boots (& so decrypts the needed partition) when nomachine it...

replies.author: @paulmaydaynight9925
@paulmaydaynight9925
replies.text:
@MUHAMMADYAWARIFRAHEEM "not everyone have modren auto detect monitor"
that's just silly since IBM licenced the uk transputer gfx ip & relabelled it SVGA back in the day

everything can work at low SVGA SVGA 800×600 uk 25/us 31ish Hz at least. today the standard is average hd 1080p 25hz/50hz including tv's used as a monitor.

oc there's also the antiquated 'old sold as new' specialty  exceptions.
"Raspberry Pi 4 Case with Touch Screen, 3.5 inch Touchscreen with Raspberry Pi Fan Raspberry Pi Heatsinks, 320x480 Monitor TFT LCD" but they disable the hdmi 1080p output so their custom driver can exclusively run that tiny screen.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
